note_to_verifier: This run performed implementation + internal self-consistency only, per invariant I4. No self-verification was performed. Independent adversarial verification (/cdr:verify) is a separate, required next step.

Catalog_type.notes: Doc comment documents the 3-lock model in full: stripes (record-level, per-fileID), indexMu (index map lookup structure), fmMu (all calls into shared *FileManager, added after a real -race failure - see below).

Put.semantics: Delete-then-reinsert on every call (documented simplicity-over-efficiency tradeoff, not in-place update). Overwrites the current record for a fileID with no history/versioning.

Get.semantics: Takes the record's stripe lock (not strictly required by Page/FileManager's own copy-on-read semantics, but taken anyway for linearizability against a concurrent Put's delete-then-reinsert sequence for the SAME fileID).

Delete.semantics: Removes from index first (under indexMu), returns ErrNotFound if absent, otherwise tombstones the underlying slot.

not_found_error.symbol: var ErrNotFound = errors.New("catalog: fileID not found")
not_found_error.usage: Wrapped with fileID context via fmt.Errorf's %w in both Get and Delete; callers should use errors.Is(err, catalog.ErrNotFound).

stripe_selection_scheme.scheme: fileID % numStripes (numStripes = 256). Deliberate plain-modulo choice, documented as acceptable because fileIDs are allocated by IDAllocator.Next() as a monotonically increasing counter (already well-distributed by allocation order); a proper hash (fnv/xxhash) would be more robust to adversarial/pathological ID patterns but is not required here.

put_simplicity_tradeoff.location: Put's doc comment, directly above func (c *Catalog) Put
put_simplicity_tradeoff.summary: Always delete-then-reinsert rather than update-in-place-if-it-fits. Documented as deliberate; in-place update is deferred to a later subtask once the compaction/page-allocation story matures (mirrors page.go's own deferred-compaction language).

fmMu_discovered_gap.location: Catalog doc comment, locking model item 3
fmMu_discovered_gap.summary: FileManager (1.1.3) has no internal locking over its shared state (highestAllocated, free-list bitmap). Initial per-page-only striping caused a real go test -race failure (read/write race on FileManager.highestAllocated between AllocatePage and ReadPage's validDataPageID). Fixed with a single Catalog-level fmMu serializing every fm.* call. See impact-analysis.json's discovered_issue_during_implementation for full detail.

idalloc_crosscheck_gap_note.summary: Cross-references (not fixes) the 1.1.4-flagged risk that NewIDAllocator has no cross-check against catalog.dat's actual max FileID if the .idalloc sidecar goes missing. Notes Catalog is a natural place a future subtask could close it.

index_rebuild_limitation.summary: NewCatalog does not scan catalog.dat on load to rebuild the in-memory fileID->location index from existing on-disk records; FileManager exposes no page-enumeration API and adding one was judged out of this subtask's impacted-modules scope. A fresh Catalog starts with an empty index. Underlying page bytes ARE durably persisted; only in-process index rediscovery across restarts is deferred.

acceptance_criteria_status: all met, per validation-matrix.json (4/4 scenarios covered and passing)
self_consistency_results: .cdr/runs/2026-07-03/013-implementation/self-consistency.json - go build ./engine/..., go vet ./engine/catalog/..., go test -run TestCatalog -race -v, and full package go test -race -v all pass.
